FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Strong Q2 bank earnings expected, but Fed rate-hike risks loom amid geopolitical tensions and mixed inflation signals.

bullets.text: Q2 earnings set to boom: SpaceX IPO advisory fees, Iran war volatility trading, and commercial lending pickup driving big bank revenue surge.
Rate hike odds rising for July on Strait of Hormuz oil spike—direct headwind to NIM compression and deposit-pricing strategies.
Fed Governor Waller signals caution: inflation may be broadening beyond energy/tariffs, keeping hike door open despite recent disinflation.
CFPB nominee Brian Johnson wins 60-day discretionary review on staff cuts post-confirmation—Trump administration gains regulatory workforce control.

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: RIA consolidation momentum accelerates with leadership transitions and channel restructuring, while retirement plan advisory gains competitive urgency.

bullets.text: Hightower hits organic growth highs as RIA M&A intensifies: ex-Fido CTO raises $7.7M for standalone platform, Choreo closes fourth deal post-SpaceX, Wealthfront crosses $100B AUM.
Aspen-backed Summitry ($4B AUM) undergoes leadership reset after 22 years—founder Higgins moves to executive chairman as President Katz and COO Wilkes become co-CEOs.
Carson Group splits go-to-market into separate W-2 and independent RIA channels to sharpen focus and drive organic growth amid consolidation competition.
401(k) plan advisory increasingly driven by revenue opportunity and competitive positioning—advisors pushing innovation to differentiate in crowded market.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: Brazil's insurance market opens for business, Mile Auto expands via acquisition, Kenvue faces major litigation resurgence, and AI tools reshape underwriting.

bullets.text: Brazil's new Insurance Law takes effect with strong reinsurer interest—Uplift CEO signals market maturation, improved claims handling, and regulatory openness attracting capital.
Mile Auto acquires Insurance House (~$100M premium, 55k policyholders) to reach 10 states and 1,600 distribution partners—consolidation play in AI-driven MGA space.
Federal appeals court revives 500+ Tylenol-autism lawsuits against Kenvue on July 13, overturning dismissal and creating significant litigation exposure.
Dun & Bradstreet integrates with Claude AI for real-time risk data in underwriting; Marsh launches MLOne umbrella facility to streamline excess casualty claims coordination.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: Mastercard divests Vocalink, CMBS distress spreads, and fintech valuations climb despite credit headwinds.

bullets.text: Mastercard exploring sale of Vocalink, the UK payments platform acquired in 2016, potentially returning to original banking consortium owners.
CMBS special servicing rate jumped in June 2026 as large retail, office, and lodging loans deteriorate—signaling credit stress and upcoming servicer workouts.
North America private debt remains LP-favored despite divergence between performing and distressed credits, per AlbaCore analysis.
Moneybox valued at £800M (45% jump since 2024) in secondary raise, while European banks adopting AI early face competitive edge over laggards by 2030.

PE & Deals.lede: Four stories spanning GP financing innovation, secondaries team builds, and strategic sector bets across defense tech and infrastructure.

bullets.text: Arctos Partners closed $6.2B GP financing using 'self-liquidating' Keystone structure—a debut deployment in the GP-financing market that could reshape how sponsors access capital.
Moelis poaching Evercore VP to build LP-led secondaries practice as the segment booms; LGT, Dawson, and Pomona already aggressively sourcing deals on favorable pricing.
Bridgepoint backing Danish defense-tech spinout UMag with majority stake while founders retain control; capital fueling international expansion amid European defense spending surge.

Markets & Macro.lede: Bank earnings season opens amid inflation uncertainty, while PE mega-funds consolidate capital and Wall Street's IB pipeline hits 4-year highs on M&A and AI momentum.

bullets.text: Fed's Waller flags rate hike possibility if this week's inflation data disappoints—market braced for earnings misses despite high expectations.
Top 20 PE firms hoarded $519B (up 27% YoY) in H1 2026, leaving smaller funds starved; two-tier consolidation risk building.
US IB fees tracking toward highest since 2022 on M&A revival and IPO recovery, but AI-related debt issuance surged 99% YoY—creating concentration red flags in fixed income.

Newsletters.lede: Big bank earnings dominate today's agenda as market flows turn negative and AI competition heats up globally.

bullets.text: Five of six megabanks report Q2 earnings tomorrow—watch credit quality, deposits, and consumer health across all major business lines.
ETF flows went negative this week in a rare reversal; redemptions hit equity, bond, and alternative products across the board.
Alibaba's AI model ranks #1 globally—cheaper Chinese alternatives are eating into US tech dominance in artificial intelligence.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: Five stories spanning AI moats, market competition theory, suburban economics, and policy literacy—mostly academic takes with limited financial market implications.

bullets.text: Open-source AI models now match frontier performance at 1/10th the cost, forcing founders to compete on moats beyond raw capability—a structural shift in software economics.
Tyler Cowen makes a neat theoretical argument: P ≠ NP preserves competitive markets by making collusion detection computationally intractable; inverse holds if P = NP.
Noah Smith defends suburbs as economically rational (affordability, schools, safety), pushing back on elite cultural dismissal—relevant context for real estate and consumer behavior.

all.lede: Bank earnings season dominates the week as mega-funds consolidate capital; Mastercard considers Vocalink exit while Wall Street banking fees hit 4-year highs.

bullets.text: Five of six largest US banks report Q2 earnings tomorrow—credit quality, deposits, and consumer health will be under the microscope across lending, trading, and wealth management.
Top 20 PE firms raised $519B in H1 2026, up 27% YoY, while smaller funds struggle; two-tier market is creating consolidation pressure among sub-scale players.
US investment banking fees tracking toward highest annual total since 2022, driven by M&A revival, IPO recovery, and AI-fueled DCM activity.
Mastercard exploring sale of Vocalink, potentially returning the UK payments platform to its original bank consortium owners a decade after acquisition.
